sanskrit: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च । अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पृथानन्दन  दम्भ करना, घमण्ड करना, अभिमान करना, क्रोध करना, कठोरता रखना और अविवेकका होना भी  ये सभी आसुरीसम्पदाको प्राप्त हुए मनुष्यके लक्षण हैं ॥
englishTranslation: O son of Pritha, hypocrisy, arrogance, self-importance, anger, harshness, and ignorance are the marks of one born with demonic qualities.
essence: False pride and cruelty reveal a nature already turned away from wisdom.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: False display, pride, self-importance, anger, harsh speech, and ignorance are signs of a destructive nature.
simpleInsight: It feels clarifying to name the habits that poison character before they harden into identity.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna has just described the qualities of a noble nature. Now he turns to Arjuna and names the opposite pattern: hypocrisy, pride, anger, cruelty, and ignorance. The contrast prepares Arjuna to see what kind of life leads to bondage.
modernRelevance: In a tense family argument, someone smiles politely while cutting you down, then explodes when challenged. The verse names that mix of pride, cruelty, and blindness for what it is.
translationLiteral: दम्भः (hypocrisy) / दर्पः (arrogance) / अभिमानः (self-importance) / च (and) / क्रोधः (anger) / पारुष्यम् (harshness) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / च (and) / अभिजातस्य (of one born) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सम्पदम् (qualities) / आसुरीम् (demonic)
insight: It teaches you to spot character by recurring reactions, not by polished appearances.

quoteVariants.text: Hypocrisy, arrogance, anger, and harshness mark a demonic nature.
Arrogance and harshness are not strength.
